markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' confirmation of Grand Theft Auto VI's November 19, 2026 release date, following multiple delays from the original 2025 window and a brief May 2026 target, has extended the timeline and sharpened trader focus on intervening events. The studio cited the need for additional polish in official statements, aligning with Take-Two Interactive's earnings commentary that the date remains firm. This six-month buffer opens space for cultural milestones like major album drops, AI advancements such as GPT-6, or geopolitical shifts, while historical patterns show Rockstar rarely alters locked dates once announced. Upcoming Take-Two updates and ongoing development reports could further clarify momentum in this extended pre-release period.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two Interactive have locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 release after two official delays, first shifting from a 2025 window to May 26, 2026 in May 2025, then pushing it six more months in November 2025 to allow extra polish on the open-world title set in Vice City. Take-Two’s earnings updates and Rockstar’s direct statements continue to reaffirm this date without signaling further slips, and as of late May 2026 no credible reports indicate movement before the June threshold. Traders see overwhelming evidence in the publisher’s consistent public commitments and the scale of post-production work required for a game of this scope. A realistic upset would require an unprecedented acceleration or surprise early access drop, both viewed as extremely remote given the fixed timeline and historical Rockstar caution on quality.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial declarations in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrial on the Jessica Mann rape charge, following a deadlocked jury on May 15, 2026, have reinforced trader consensus around limited additional prison exposure. With the 73-year-old already having served over six years and facing appeals on his 16-year California conviction plus a split New York verdict, prosecutors’ decision on pursuing another retrial after the June 24 hearing introduces uncertainty but favors outcomes where new sentences run concurrent or credit against time served. Historical patterns in similar high-profile cases and the lower-level nature of remaining charges further support the market’s emphasis on no net additional incarceration as the dominant implied probability.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final matchup between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights has driven the current trader consensus, with Carolina positioned as the slight favorite entering the series. Carolina advanced by defeating the Montreal Canadiens in five games to reach its first Final since winning the Cup in 2006, extending a dominant playoff run that includes strong recent form and home-ice advantage for Game 1. Vegas earned its spot by sweeping the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Final, marking the franchise's return to the championship round for the first time since its 2023 title, fueled by a strong stretch of results following a late-March coaching change. These conference outcomes and respective momentum have shaped the implied probabilities reflected in the market.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold the lead in 2026 NBA champion markets after defeating the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder in a seven-game Western Conference Finals series, capped by a Game 7 road victory. Victor Wembanyama’s two-way dominance and the team’s 62-20 regular-season record have driven trader consensus toward San Antonio, reflecting its deeper roster health and recent momentum against top competition. The New York Knicks, who swept the Cleveland Cavaliers to claim the Eastern Conference title, sit second on the strength of their offensive execution led by Jalen Brunson and a franchise-first Finals appearance since 1999. Their hot postseason streak and roster construction through free agency and trades keep implied probabilities competitive, though the Spurs enter the best-of-seven series as the clear favorite per current pricing.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit nearly tied atop the 2026 World Cup winner market, reflecting their status as the top two sides in the latest FIFA rankings and their proven depth across recent cycles. Spain enters on the back of strong European Championship performances and consistent qualification results, while France maintains elite attacking and defensive options despite transitional phases. England follows closely, buoyed by back-to-back major final appearances and a settled core, with Brazil, Argentina, and Portugal rounding out the leading group on the strength of historical pedigree and current form. The expanded 48-team field and completed qualification window have kept probabilities clustered, as multiple European and South American contenders carry comparable squad quality and scheduling advantages heading into the June-July tournament in North America.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through ongoing consolidation measures, including the 2018 removal of presidential term limits and repeated high-level purges. Recent actions, such as the February 2026 expulsion of 19 National People's Congress deputies and senior generals from the People's Liberation Army, have been viewed as efforts to eliminate potential rival networks rather than signs of vulnerability. No designated successor has emerged, and Xi continues to exercise authority in public diplomacy, including statements on U.S.-China relations in May 2026. These factors underpin trader consensus that he will complete his current term and seek a fourth at the 2027 party congress, with limited credible challenges materializing in the interim.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the trader consensus in this wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his term-limited status as California governor, sustained national visibility through public engagement with the Trump administration, and early positioning moves including donor outreach. The fragmented field shows limited consolidation behind alternatives, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez drawing progressive support, Kamala Harris benefiting from recent general-election experience, and governors such as Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg appealing to moderates in early polling. Key variables that could shift probabilities include 2026 midterm results, early-state polling trends, fundraising totals, and potential endorsements from party leaders ahead of the first primaries. No candidate has formally entered the race.
